heroHeadline: Kansas City Police Launch 911 Translation for World Cup Visitors — 58 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: Kansas City Police Department has launched a real-time 911 translation service enabling emergency dispatchers to communicate with non-English-speaking visitors ahead of World Cup matches at Arrowhead Stadium. The initiative is part of KC's broader host-city preparedness effort and marks a model rollout for multilingual emergency services across other host cities. With 58 days to the June 11 opener, host cities are accelerating public safety and visitor services preparations as international fans begin travel planning.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, Inside World Football
